entries.summary: Holds GitHub-hosted collaboration scaffolding such as issue forms and the pull request template.
Defines the repository-wide wrapper instructions and routes work into shared reusable-core workflow surfaces.
Describes the purpose of the foundations directory and the intended read order across its documents.
Holds shared implementation assets, shared docs, and the authored control-plane tree.
Describes the purpose of the core root and its main subtrees.
Holds authoritative machine-readable schemas, registries, contracts, indexes, examples, and retained records.
Defines the local instruction layer for authored control-plane artifacts and machine-governed boundary work.
Describes the purpose, boundaries, and main artifact families in the control-plane tree.
Versioned machine-readable contract families, currently the acceptance-contract set.
Describes the purpose of the contracts directory and its main contract families.
Defines machine-readable acceptance contracts derived from durable initiative or promoted guidance acceptance criteria.
Describes the purpose of the acceptance contracts directory and its current contents.
Durable generic acceptance-contract example used by command docs, standards, and validation tests in the engineering repository; portable exports intentionally scrub it.
Deliberate derived lookup files when a concrete consumer exists.

Machine-readable command indexes for command lookup and routing to command pages.

Unified traceability indexes that join planning, contract, validator, and evidence surfaces.

Canonical lookup data and pack-startup registries owned by core.

Entry page for shared governed query commands such as authority resolution, command discovery, repository paths, templates, standards, references, foundations, workflows, acceptance, evidence, and benchmarks.
Resolves which shared governed surface is canonical for a recurring governance question.
Searches retained benchmark records captured by the reusable-core benchmarking runtime.

Runs the local release gate and stages one final export.

Runs one pack-declared validation suite through the reusable-core suite runtime.

Holds shared fixture data for tests when authored examples are not sufficient.
Describes the purpose of the Python test-fixtures directory.

Neutral synthetic fixture pack used to materialize temporary hosted-pack repos for shared-core validation scenarios. Tests should treat this as template data rather than as a required live-pack import contract. The materializer derives pack identity from the target pack root by default, so new shared-core tests should not hard-code `plan` or any other live pack unless the behavior under test truly requires that exact pack.
Holds integration tests that exercise multiple package surfaces together.
Describes the purpose of the integration-test directory.
Shared JSON and front-matter helpers for the focused integration artifact suites.
